trivia_bullets.text: Common Horror from Modern Horizons 3 (MH3), released June 14, 2024 — a draft-innovation set injecting cards directly into the Modern card pool.

"When the night comes knocking / —Kessig expression meaning 'in dire times'" — attributed to Kessig idiom, not a named speaker. Kessig is the forested, werewolf-haunted province of Innistrad (a separate plane from where MH3 is set). The attribution is a cross-plane flavor tag borrowing from Innistrad's hardscrabble rural folk culture.
sources: Wizards.com: Planeswalker's Guide to Innistrad: Kessig and Werewolves

trivia_bullets.text: Rated 1.5/5 in MH3 limited by MTG Arena Zone: 'a solid top end card that can potentially rack up some real card advantage' but vulnerable to aggressive pressure before Adapt activates. Adapt is a Simic mechanic originally from Ravnica Allegiance; its presence here on a mono-black Horror is one of MH3's cross-guild mechanical transplants.
sources: MTG Arena Zone: MH3 Limited Set Review — Black

related_cards.name: Dreamdrinker Vampire
related_cards.note: Fellow black Adapt common in MH3; shares the adapt-triggers-bonus pattern

related_cards.name: Emperor of Bones
related_cards.note: Black Adapt payoff at uncommon in the same set; rated strongest of the MH3 black Adapt cycle in limited reviews

related_cards.name: Kessig Wolf Run
related_cards.note: Canonical Kessig-plane card; the flavor text's geographic attribution connects back to Innistrad's werewolf province
